bullets.text: Q1 profitability in-line with targets despite early spring hail; Direct auto running combined ratio near 96 maximum given front-loaded acquisition costs and 4% average premium growth; Agency and Commercial Lines performing within bounds; March CR 7.6 pts worse YOY primarily from weather.

Three consecutive months of mid-teens premium growth; crossed 10 million auto policies in force (16.5 million vehicles); total policies exceeded 15 million including special, commercial, and property; Direct Personal Lines and Commercial Lines each growing 20%+.

Agency auto growth returning to 6% range after years of disappointment; Platinum agency program building Robinson momentum; retention leading indicators now converting to measurable PLE improvement; Robinson retention gains multi-year in nature.

Snapshot redesign in state expansion; Snapshot mobile app announced for 2016 launch (smartphone-based UBI); Uber pilot launched in Texas — first TNC coverage relationship; designed as 3-phase coverage solution developed collaboratively with Uber and regulators.

Q1 investment return 1.2% FTE; portfolio ~$21B; strong capital position entering year with $400M debt offering completed in 2015; debt-to-capital near 27%; strong underwriting capital generation supporting growth capacity.

Early spring hail in hail belt created Property losses; special lines favorable with no major storm; Snapshot data used to distinguish weather-driven frequency from underlying trend in real-time; El Niño weather effects actively modeled using 100s of weather monitoring stations.

Strategy of continuous small rate adjustments state-by-state to minimize consumer disruption and preserve retention; auto products likely need continued rate to match expected future trends; average written premium growing ~4% in personal auto, ~13% in commercial.

Commercial Lines competitive market conditions with fewer competitors bidding in select segments; agency market showing Progressive product attractiveness at current rate levels; Platinum offering raising Progressive profile among targeted Platinum agents.

Snapshot smartphone app targeted for 2016 to reach broader policyholder base with data integrity standards finally met; Uber TNC pilot data collection off to strong start (coincided with Final Four basketball in Houston); Progressive viewing vehicles as 'moving IP addresses.'

ASI Property integration enabling Platinum bundled offering in Agency — single-provider with coordinated policy periods and compensation; Progressive Advantage Agency expanding in Direct; Direct and Agency auto channels roughly equally balanced at 50/50 split.
